items.title: Missing votes counted in key SA seat see One Nation margin increase

children.content: One Nation has retained the South Australian seat of Narungga, following a count of ballots that were missed on election day.

articles.title: Uncovered ballots prompt new count in key SA seat won by One Nation

children.content: The Electoral Commission of South Australia has uncovered more than 600 ballot papers that were not counted in the recent state election, including dozens from the closely contested seat of Narungga on the Yorke Peninsula.

image.altText: A composite image of two women with blonde hair

gallery.altText: One Nation's Chantelle Thomas at a media conference.

thumbnail.altText: One Nation's Chantelle Thomas at a media conference.

items.title: Bomb squad removes 'volatile' explosives from Adelaide home, police say

children.content: Police say the bomb squad has now safely removed chemicals from the suburban Adelaide home of a 55-year-old man who has been charged with explosives offences, but say the matter is not terror-related.

image.altText: The South Australia Police logo on a building.

items.title: Bandicoots bounce back in Brown Hill Creek as team works towards 'superhighway'

children.content: A small colony of endangered brown bandicoots just south of Adelaide continue to breed as conservationists hope to connect them with other populations.

image.altText: Southern brown bandicoot, Isoodon obesulus obesulus

items.title: BreastScreen SA did not get ethics approval or consent before research, ombudsman finds

children.content: South Australia's public breast cancer screening service conducted research without ethics approval or consent from almost 100 patients, who it asked to do a second breast needle biopsy, prompting the service to "unreservedly" apologise.

gallery.altText: The office of BreastScreen SA in Adelaide.

thumbnail.altText: The office of BreastScreen SA in Adelaide.

items.title: Airport workers angered by surprise Qantas route cuts

children.content: Qantas's decision to cut one regional South Australian route and suspend four others came as a shock to workers at regional airports.

image.altText: A QantasLink plane on the tarmac

items.title: 'Attention-seeking' man allegedly targeted police, defence in 'cybercrime spree'

children.content: A 22-year-old Adelaide man who allegedly went on a "cybercrime spree", targeting several government departments and a courthouse, will remain behind bars until at the least the end of the year.

image.altText: Close up image of the face of a man with Asian heritage

items.title: 'No-one's committed murder here': Port CEO urges change to tribunal process

children.content: Port Adelaide's chief executive has slammed the AFL Tribunal process, labelling it "too legalistic" and "too adversarial".

image.altText: A man is a suit is frowning as he looks toward his left side.

items.title: SA government has 'assurances' from LIV Golf as course upgrade under fresh scrutiny

children.content: The South Australian government has reaffirmed its commitment to a $45 million upgrade of the North Adelaide public golf course to host LIV Golf in 2028, and has "assurances" about the tour's future.

image.altText: Two flags on a golf course with large buildings from the city in the background.

items.title: Australia's rarest reptile found after being considered extinct

children.content: Not found anywhere else on Earth, a skink considered functionally extinct has been found in a national park in far west NSW.

image.altText: A skink on a large rock

items.title: SA government apologises to prisoner denied abortion medication

children.content: The South Australian ombudsman calls for changes to medical monitoring at the Adelaide Women's Prison after a female prisoner was denied a medical abortion, which the health minister says is unacceptable.

image.altText: A building surrounded by a fence and trees

items.title: Government advisory group urges delay of NDIS 'robo' changes

children.content: The federal government has been urged by its own NDIS advisory group to delay the rollout of a controversial new way of determining support plans for participants.

image.altText: An NDIS logo is seen on a building

items.title: Pacific leaders declare emergency over potential fuel shortages

children.content: The prime minister of Solomon Islands says Pacific leaders have declared an emergency over the looming threat of fuel shortages, while the Australian government contemplates a plan which could see stocks of diesel held by the US military sent to Pacific nations suffering shortages.

image.altText: A petrol station at night with vehicles lining up to fill up.

items.title: Europe was a word British PMs dared not say. Then Donald Trump returned

children.content: Keir Starmer was never a fan of Brexit. Now, under the cover of the war in Iran and a fractured US relationship, he's looking to Europe to return Britain to the fold.

image.altText: Keir Starmer speaks in the House of Commons

avatar.alt: Brett Worthington stands in a courtyard inside parliament house

items.title: Australian woman killed in alleged DV murder in the US

children.content: A woman from the NSW Central Coast has died from stab wounds allegedly inflicted by her husband at their home in the US state of Florida.

image.altText: A woman with short blonde hair wearing a black hat, she is smiling.

items.title: Chinese device found in strategic waters near Bali identified

children.content: A large torpedo-shaped device discovered near an island in a strategically important strait between Bali and Lombok has been identified by defence analysts as a Chinese undersea monitoring system.

gallery.altText: A torpedo-like object in the shallows at a beach.

thumbnail.altText: A torpedo-like object in the shallows at a beach.

items.title: Daughter tells jury she saw mum crush drugs into lethal smoothie

children.content: The daughter of a Gold Coast woman accused of killing her son alleges she saw her mum use a hammer to crush up drugs before putting them into a lethal fruit smoothie.

image.altText: A woman with sunglasses and wearing a pink and white shirt.

items.title: Tasmanian MP thankful to survive after crash with cow on highway

children.content: Tasmania's former deputy premier has thanked God after his car struck a "black cow" on a major highway at night.

image.altText: Michael Ferguson at his resignation press conference.

items.title: Dogs coach laments 'traumatic' night as Swans overcome GWS and lightning

children.content: The Bulldogs are trounced by 75 points and lose Sam Darcy to a knee injury as Sydney retains its supremacy against GWS. Follow live.

image.altText: A group of AFL players celebrate a goal

items.title: Panthers win golden point thriller but lose key man Kenny

children.content: Penrith have paid a heavy price for their extra time win over the Dolphins with hooker Mitch Kenny facing a lengthy stint on the sidelines.

image.altText: Mitch Kenny grimaces after an injury

items.title: AI Val Kilmer branded 'terrifying', but filmmakers say movie is ethical

children.content: A trailer for a film with an AI-generated character in the likeness of the late Val Kilmer has been slammed by social media users, but the filmmakers are defending their work.

gallery.altText: An AI-generated image of Val Kilmer with his hands on the shoulders of a girl with pigtails

thumbnail.altText: An AI-generated image of Val Kilmer with his hands on the shoulders of a girl with pigtails

items.title: How mobile phone cameras have helped unearth a mysterious kingdom

children.content: An exhibition bridges the gap between art and science by inviting visitors to imagine the world through the senses of other species and consider our own fragile relationship with nature.

image.altText: A cluster of cup-shaped mushrooms cling to a damp fallen branch amonst feathery ferns.

items.title: Weekly news quiz: Trump's AI image, a 200m record and the Artemis II splashdown

children.content: It's Friday, and that means it's time to test your news knowledge from the past seven days. Were you paying attention this week?

image.altText: A man and a woman in NASA space suits, wearing caps saying Artemis 2 smile at the camera

items.title: Travellers in Europe may face major flight disruption over jet fuel crisis

children.content: Europe could experience major flight disruptions within weeks as global energy officials warn the continent has "maybe six weeks of jet fuel left", which could lead to further price hikes and cancellations for Australian travellers.

gallery.altText: A woman stands at the British Airways check-in counter.

thumbnail.altText: A woman stands at the British Airways check-in counter.

avatar.alt: A white woman with long blonde hair sitting in a TV studio and smiling

items.title: Oil refinery fire will not push Australia to fuel plan 'stage three', PM says

children.content: Anthony Albanese has cut a trip to Malaysia short to visit Viva Energy Refinery in Geelong, which went up in flames late on Wednesday.

image.altText: A close up of Albanese, who wears a neutral expression

items.title: 'Australians deserve to know': Victorian political donations site goes dark

children.content: Victoria has been left without laws to govern party donation disclosures — exposing the state's politics to a flood of unchecked donations.

image.altText: Victorian Premier Jacinta Allan touches her glasses while at a press conference.

items.title: Mayor fears hidden cameras may have been in town hall change rooms for years

children.content: The Horsham mayor is worried that hidden cameras reportedly found in the town hall theatre change rooms could have been there for years.

image.altText: Inside Horsham Town Hall foyer, turquoise wall in background, chairs and tables in foreground

items.title: Mental health nurse who raped teen patient, filmed 'upskirting' videos jailed

children.content: A former West Australian mental health nurse is sentenced to 12 years in jail after being convicted for persistent sexual abuse of a teenager and making "upskirting" videos of women and children.

image.altText: Adam McGovern wears a hat, face mask and dark jacket as he walks outside the District Court.

items.title: Custom thongs, hugs and selfies: Harry and Meghan's very Sydney visit

children.content: The Duke and Duchess of Sussex spoke with lifeguards who responded to the December 14 Bondi Beach terror attack and survivors.

gallery.altText: Harry and Meghan pose with a group of surf life savers inside a surf club.
Harry holds up thongs that were given to him as a gift during his sydney visit
Prince Harry meets with Invictus Australia veteran, Joel Vanderzwan accompanied by his family

thumbnail.altText: Harry and Meghan pose with a group of surf life savers inside a surf club.
Harry holds up thongs that were given to him as a gift during his sydney visit
Prince Harry meets with Invictus Australia veteran, Joel Vanderzwan accompanied by his family

items.title: Former middle school teacher jailed for striking student in the face

children.content: The court heard Darroch Robinson struck the girl after she insulted him following the confiscation of her phone, causing her to fall into a table and break her teeth.

image.altText: A man in a shirt and tie leaves court

items.title: Uncle 'worried and scared' after fatally stabbing sistergirl, court hears

children.content: The partner of a man charged with the stabbing murder of a Tiwi Islands sistergirl tells the NT Supreme Court she saw him run outside "like a flash" before crying and repeatedly calling out "call the police".

image.altText: A First Nations man wearing mirrored sunglasses, pointing at himself.

items.title: What we know about the 'small fire' that became an 'unprecedented' Geelong refinery blaze

children.content: The "unprecedented" fire at one of the two remaining oil refineries in Australia took more than 12 hours to extinguish. Here's what we know about how it unfolded.

articles.title: Queensland government in discussions for new oil refinery

children.content: As the fuel crisis continues, a proposal for an $11 billion new oil refinery in central Queensland has been put forward.

image.altText: A close up of the exhaust pipes at a petrol refinery.

items.title: 'Never thought we'd be homeless': Mother of 13 pleads for help

children.content: Finding a home for a large family in a regional area has left one family camping on the side of the highway in rural New South Wales. Nationally, Indigenous people are over-represented in homelessness statistics.

image.altText: A boy eats his noodles in a camp chair.

items.title: Companies and directors fined $7m over payday lending scheme

children.content: The fines were issued against an operation that breached credit rules and generated more than $91 million in fees and charges.

image.altText: Hand holding a mobile phone with the Cigno app showing words: Why choose Cigno?

items.title: Australia is on the verge of aged care AI boom

children.content: Artificial intelligence-enabled robot companions, personal behaviour monitors and pain management apps could soon be used more widely within the sector.

gallery.altText: A humanoid robot that appears as a brunette female, offering its right hand while wearing a blue 'DexLab' shirt.

thumbnail.altText: A humanoid robot that appears as a brunette female, offering its right hand while wearing a blue 'DexLab' shirt.
